  [@ferretti_sex_2018; @mielke_consideration_2022], differential APOE ε4
  effects, and hormonal influences on vascular
  regulation may all contribute to the observed
  sex asymmetry in CVR-brain relationships
- Covariates (age, education, APOE ε4) exhibited
  expected effects across all models
  (Appendix B, @tbl-lme-covariates and
  @tbl-cvr-lme-covariates)

## HVR-Cognitive Coupling

- The HVR slope → Cognitive slope relationship
  (b-path) was consistent regardless of whether FRS
  or CVR~mimic~ was the predictor (@fig-mediation)
- Significant b-path estimates appeared in Memory
  and Executive Function for both predictors (4/6
  each; Language non-significant), supporting HVR
  decline as a reliable marker of cognitive decline
  [@kramer_longitudinal_2007; @oschwald_brain_2020],
  extending earlier evidence that HVR captures
  hippocampal integrity more sensitively than raw
  volume [@schoemaker_hippocampal-ventricle_2019;
  @fernandez-lozano_enhanced_2025;
  @bruno_hippocampal_2016]
- Parallel process coupling showed a complementary
  pattern: significant in all male models (3/3) but
  only Executive Function in females (1/3). This
  sex asymmetry likely reflects lower statistical
  power in the smaller female subsample rather than
  a biological absence of coupling, given that
  mediation b-paths (which condition on a predictor)
  were significant in 4/6 models for both sexes
- Language b-paths were non-significant across both
  analysis frameworks (coupling and mediation),
  consistent with language being a later-declining
  domain with less variance to capture at this
  disease stage
- The hippocampal-cognitive coupling is not an
  artifact of CVR measurement — it persists across
  predictor specifications and analysis frameworks

## Methodological Considerations

- The fixed-quadratic LGCM captures population-level
  curvature but constrains individual quadratic
  variation, so slope estimates primarily reflect
  average rates of change. This makes the a-path
  null finding conservative — if CVR~mimic~ had
  individual-level non-linear effects on hippocampal
  trajectories, these would require a fully random
  quadratic model to detect, further strengthening
  the conclusion that the FRS a-path reflects
  age-weighting
- EDT linearity tests confirmed significant
  non-linear trajectories across all models
  (Appendix B, @tbl-linearity), motivating the
  fixed-quadratic specification [@newsom_longitudinal_2015].
  Fully random quadratic parallel process models
  failed to converge due to near-zero quadratic
  variance and insufficient data capacity for
  the combined model complexity.
  The use of EDT rather than calendar time as the
  LGCM time metric accounts for heterogeneous
  disease progression rates across participants,
  though it introduces dependence on the progmod
  estimation
- Bootstrap CIs confirmed that most b-path effects
  were significant (4/6 for both FRS and CVR~mimic~;
  CIs excluding zero), consistent with nested LRT
  results. Language b-paths were non-significant,
  likely reflecting lower statistical power for
  this domain
- **Sensitivity summary:**
  - HC z-score effects were weaker than HVR z-score
    effects (Appendix C, @tbl-hc-comparison),
    supporting the
    ratio measure's greater sensitivity
  - Systematic baseline differences between completers
    and dropouts (Appendix C,
    @tbl-attrition-baseline) suggest
    observed effects may underestimate true CVR-brain
    relationships
  - Raw (un-normalized) HVR and HC results were
    concordant with z-scored analyses, confirming the
    normative transformation does not drive findings
  - Limited EDT moderation suggests CVR effects are
    stable across disease stages
  - SE-constrained LGCM validation showed comparable
    bias, RMSE, and coverage to free estimation

## Clinical Implications

- In clinical trials targeting cardiovascular risk
  reduction for dementia prevention, FRS should not
  be used as the sole CVR endpoint in elderly samples
  due to age-related variance compression and
  age-confounding
  [@ko_calibration_2020; @marrugat_adaptation_2003]
- Age-adjusted CVR measures (via MIMIC models or
  residualization) should be considered when the
  research question concerns cardiovascular effects
  independent of aging [@zhang_bayesian_2024]
- HVR provides a more sensitive neuroimaging endpoint
  than hippocampal volume alone, and the consistent
  HVR-cognition coupling (b-path) supports its use
  as a surrogate outcome in intervention studies
- The female CVR~mimic~ Executive Function LME
  3-way interaction was the sole significant
  CVR~mimic~ moderation result out of 6 tests
  (2 sexes × 3 domains), and did not survive FDR
  correction. This isolated finding should be
  treated as exploratory and requires independent
  replication before any clinical interpretation

## Limitations

- Single cohort (ADNI); replication in other aging
  samples needed. The UKB-trained GAMLSS normative
  models showed a systematic negative z-score offset
  in the ADNI comparable subsample, driven by age
  distribution mismatch. While within-ADNI relative
  comparisons remain valid, absolute z-score values
  should be interpreted cautiously when comparing
  across cohorts
- CVR~mimic~ indicators limited to available CVRF
  data; more comprehensive indicators (e.g., lipid
  panel, inflammatory markers) could improve the
  latent CVR measure
- Cross-sectional CVR measurement; longitudinal CVR
  trajectories could be informative
- Amyloid-positive sample only; findings may not
  generalize to cognitively normal individuals
  without AD pathology
- The formal coefficient comparison (shared-seed
  bootstrap Δ~a~ and clustered bootstrap Δβ) relies
  on the same resampling framework as the primary
  mediation inference; independent validation of
  these paired tests is not available
- Female CVR~mimic~ Executive LME effect requires
  replication to determine whether it reflects true
  CVR moderation or a chance finding
- The fixed-quadratic LGCM captures population-level
  curvature but constrains individual quadratic
  variation; slope estimates may still obscure
  individual-level acceleration or deceleration
  patterns
- LGCM models used individually-varying time scores
  (EDT definition variables), which preclude
  computation of CFI, TLI, and RMSEA — we assessed
  model adequacy via information criteria and
  nested LRTs only, limiting comparability with
  studies reporting standard fit indices
- MIMIC model used only 5 indicators; richer
  measurement models may yield different results.
  Factor scores are model-dependent and different
  specifications could yield different CVR~mimic~
  values
- Common-sample filtering may reduce N relative to
  analyses using only one CVR measure, potentially
  limiting power
- Bootstrap mediation CIs (N =
  `r med_n.lst$Male` males,
  `r med_n.lst$Female` females) may be liberal with
  these modest sample sizes; results should be
  interpreted with caution

## Conclusions

- Whether CVR appears to influence
  hippocampal-cognitive trajectories depends
  critically on how CVR is measured: FRS produces
  significant indirect effects through hippocampal
  decline, while age-adjusted CVR~mimic~ does not
- This dissociation supports the hypothesis that FRS
  "effects" on hippocampal decline primarily reflect
  age-related variance rather than cardiovascular
  pathophysiology
- Suggestive single-domain evidence (Female Executive
  LME 3-way) hints that age-adjusted CVR may still
  modulate the brain-cognition relationship through
  reserve-like mechanisms
- The hippocampal-cognitive coupling persists
  regardless of CVR measurement, supporting HVR as
  a reliable biomarker target
- Future directions include replication in
  independent aging cohorts (e.g., WRAP, AIBL),
  longitudinal CVR measurement to capture
  within-person CVR trajectories, and intervention
  studies testing whether CVR reduction preserves
  the HVR-cognition coupling
- This study demonstrates a general methodological
  framework — MIMIC-based age adjustment — that can
  be applied to any composite risk score where age
  confounding is a concern

---

\newpage

:::{=latex}
\appendix
\setcounter{table}{0}
\setcounter{figure}{0}
\renewcommand{\thetable}{A\arabic{table}}
\renewcommand{\thefigure}{A\arabic{figure}}
:::

# Normative Transfer Validation


- For transfer validation, we identified a comparable
  ADNI subsample of `r adni_demo$n_total`
  amyloid-negative cognitively unimpaired participants
  (`r sprintf("%.0f%%", adni_demo$pct_female)`
  female) within the UKB age range
- Despite matching on amyloid status and cognitive normality, the
  ADNI comparable subsample was approximately
  `r sprintf("%.0f", adni_demo$age_mean - ukb_demo$age_mean)`
  years older on average (mean
  `r sprintf("%.1f", adni_demo$age_mean)`
  vs
  `r sprintf("%.1f", ukb_demo$age_mean)`
  years; @fig-normative-validation A), concentrating in the upper
  tail of the UKB age distribution
- HVR z-scores showed a systematic negative offset (mean =
  `r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hvr$mean)`,
  SD =
  `r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hvr$sd)`,
  95% CI
  [`r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hvr$ci_95["lower"])`,
  `r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hvr$ci_95["upper"])`]),
  while HC z-scores were closer to expected values (mean =
  `r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hc$mean)`,
  SD =
  `r sprintf("%.2f", comp_hc$sd)`;
  @fig-normative-validation B)
- Age-bin-specific calibration (see @fig-normative-validation, panel B):
  mean z-scores near zero in younger bins, increasingly
  negative offset in older bins consistent with the age
  mismatch

```{r fig-normative-validation}
#| fig-cap: "Normative transfer validation. (A) Age distributions: UK Biobank normative sample (yellow) vs ADNI comparable subsample (cyan); dashed lines indicate sample means. (B) Forest plot of HVR (purple) and HC (green) z-scores (mean +/- 95% CI) by age bin; reference line at z = 0 indicates perfect calibration; open circles = age bins, filled circles = overall. (C) Z-score distributions for HVR (left) and HC (right) compared to the expected N(0,1) (yellow)."
#| fig-width: 6.5
#| fig-height: 6

stopifnot(!is.null(nv_data))
ukb_d <- nv_data$comparable_subsample$demographics$ukb_normative
adni_d <- nv_data$comparable_subsample$demographics$adni_comparable

p_age <- plot_age_distribution_comparison(
  ukb_age_mean = ukb_d$age_mean,
  ukb_age_sd = ukb_d$age_sd,
  ukb_age_min = ukb_d$age_min,
  ukb_age_max = ukb_d$age_max,
  ukb_n = ukb_d$n_total,
  adni_age_mean = adni_d$age_mean,
  adni_age_sd = adni_d$age_sd,
  adni_n = adni_d$n_total
)

p_forest <- plot_zscore_validation_forest(nv_data)

# Build z-score data.table with both measures
hvr_z.v <- comp_hvr$zscores
hc_z.v <- comp_hc$zscores
stopifnot(!is.null(hvr_z.v), !is.null(hc_z.v))
n_max <- max(length(hvr_z.v), length(hc_z.v))
zscore.dt <- data.table(
  PTID = seq_len(n_max),
  HVR_z = hvr_z.v[seq_len(n_max)],
  HC_z = hc_z.v[seq_len(n_max)]
)

p_dist <- plot_zscore_distribution(
  zscore.dt, sample_label = "Comparable"
)

(p_age | p_forest) / p_dist +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
```


:::{=latex}
\FloatBarrier
\newpage
\setcounter{table}{0}
\setcounter{figure}{0}
\renewcommand{\thetable}{B\arabic{table}}
\renewcommand{\thefigure}{B\arabic{figure}}
:::

# Methodological Validations

## CVR MIMIC Measurement Invariance

Measurement invariance of the CVR MIMIC model was
tested across sex in four steps (configural, metric,
scalar, strict; @tbl-mi).

```{r tbl-mi}
#| tbl-cap: "Measurement invariance of the CVR MIMIC model across sex. Configural = same structure; Metric = equal loadings; Scalar = equal intercepts; Strict = equal residuals. ΔCFI < 0.01 and ΔRMSEA < 0.015 indicate acceptable invariance."

stopifnot(!is.null(cvr_mi.res))
require_output(
  create_mi_table(
    cvr_mi.res,
    "Measurement Invariance Across Sex"
  ),
  "tbl-mi"
)
```

## EDT Linearity and Simulation Validation

- Linear time specification validated via LME
  likelihood ratio tests comparing linear vs
  quadratic EDT across all sex-domain models
  (see @tbl-linearity)

```{r tbl-linearity}
#| tbl-cap: "EDT linearity validation: LME models testing linear vs quadratic EDT specification. LRT tests whether adding a quadratic EDT term significantly improves fit. ΔAIC = AIC(linear) - AIC(quadratic); positive values favor quadratic. EDT = Estimated Disease Time."

require_output(
  create_linearity_table(
    edt_linearity,
    title = "EDT Linearity Validation"
  ),
  "tbl-linearity"
)
```

```{r edt-linearity-narrative}
#| output: asis

stopifnot(!is.null(edt_linearity))
lt <- as.data.table(
  edt_linearity$results_table
)
# Column is LRT_p, not p
n_sig <- sum(
  lt$LRT_p < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE
)
n_total <- nrow(lt)
if (n_sig == 0) {
  cat(paste0(
    "- No domain showed a significant",
    " quadratic EDT term (all p > 0.05),",
    " supporting the linear time",
    " specification for the LGCM\n"
  ))
} else {
  sig_doms <- lt[LRT_p < 0.05, Domain]
  max_chisq <- max(
    lt$LRT_chisq, na.rm = TRUE
  )
  cat(sprintf(
    paste0(
      "- All %d/%d models showed",
      " significant quadratic EDT terms",
      " (%s; max \u03c7\u00b2 = %.1f)\n"
    ),
    n_sig, n_total,
    paste(
      DOMAIN_LABELS[sig_doms],
      collapse = ", "
    ),
    max_chisq
  ))
  cat(paste0(
    "- Large test statistics reflect",
    " substantial power afforded by",
    " sample size and repeated",
    " observations\n",
    "- Linear specification adopted as",
    " pragmatic compromise given",
    " convergence failure of quadratic",
    " parallel process LGCMs",
    " (see Methods)\n"
  ))
}
```

## SE-Constrained LGCM Validation

- Monte Carlo simulation
  (`r simulation.res$config$n_reps` replications
  at N = `r paste(simulation.res$config$sample_sizes, collapse = " and N = ")`)
  compared SE-constrained LGCM (residual variance
  fixed to SE²) against free estimation

```{r simulation-interpretation}
#| output: asis

stopifnot(!is.null(sim_conv))
fmt_sci <- function(x) {
  if (abs(x) < 1e-10) return("0")
  exp <- floor(log10(abs(x)))
  mant <- x / 10^exp
  sprintf("%.1f \u00d7 10^%d^", mant, exp)
}
for (i in seq_len(nrow(sim_conv))) {
  n_val <- sim_conv$n[i]
  cat(sprintf(
    paste0(
      "- N = %d: SE-constrained converged",
      " %d/500 (free: %d/500); bias = %s",
      " (free: %s); RMSE = %.3f",
      " (free: %.3f); 95%% CI coverage",
      " = %.1f%% (free: %.1f%%)\n"
    ),
    n_val,
    sim_conv$n_converged_se[i],
    sim_conv$n_converged_free[i],
    fmt_sci(sim_conv$bias_se[i]),
    fmt_sci(sim_conv$bias_free[i]),
    sim_conv$rmse_se[i],
    sim_conv$rmse_free[i],
    sim_conv$coverage_se[i] * 100,
    sim_conv$coverage_free[i] * 100
  ))
}
cat(paste0(
  "- Both approaches showed comparable",
  " performance, validating the",
  " SE-constrained specification used",
  " in the primary analyses\n"
))
```

## LME Covariate Effects

Full covariate effects from the sex-stratified
LME 3-way interaction models are presented for
the FRS model (@tbl-lme-covariates) and the
CVR~mimic~ model (@tbl-cvr-lme-covariates).

```{r tbl-lme-covariates}
#| tbl-cap: "Covariate effects from the FRS LME models, sex-stratified. Bold indicates p < 0.05."

require_output(
  create_lme_covariate_table(
    frs_hvr, cvr_label = "FRS",
    title = "Covariate Effects (FRS Model)"
  ),
  "tbl-lme-covariates"
)
```

```{r tbl-cvr-lme-covariates}
#| tbl-cap: "Covariate effects from the CVR~mimic~ LME models, sex-stratified. Bold indicates p < 0.05."

require_output(
  create_lme_covariate_table(
    cvr_hvr,
    cvr_label = "CVR~mimic~",
    title = md(
      "Covariate Effects (CVR~mimic~ Model)"
    )
  ),
  "tbl-cvr-lme-covariates"
)
```


:::{=latex}
\FloatBarrier
\newpage
\setcounter{table}{0}
\setcounter{figure}{0}
\renewcommand{\thetable}{C\arabic{table}}
\renewcommand{\thefigure}{C\arabic{figure}}
:::

# Sensitivity Analyses

All sensitivity checks converge on the same
pattern: FRS effects trace to age-weighting,
CVR~mimic~ effects are null or isolated, and the
HVR–cognition coupling (b-path) is consistent. Main
findings are stable across alternative brain
measures (HC volume), raw scores, attrition
adjustments, and EDT specification checks. Full
details follow.

## LME Sensitivity

### HC z-score (Total Hippocampal Volume)

- HC z-score effects were generally weaker than HVR
  z-score effects, with fewer significant 3-way
  interactions, supporting the hippocampus-to-ventricle
  ratio as a more sensitive brain aging biomarker
- LME 3-way interaction repeated with HC z-score
  instead of HVR
- Results in @tbl-hc-comparison; covariate effects in
  @tbl-hc-covariates and @tbl-hc-covariates-cvr

```{r tbl-hc-comparison}
#| tbl-cap: "Sensitivity analysis: LME 3-way interaction effects (YRS × CVR × HC z-score) for total hippocampal volume. FRS and CVR~mimic~ compared side-by-side. Bold indicates p < 0.05."

require_output(
  create_lme_comparison_table(
    frs_hc, cvr_hc,
    summary_dt = hc_summary.dt
  ),
  "tbl-hc-comparison"
)
```

```{r tbl-hc-covariates}
#| tbl-cap: "Covariate effects from the FRS HC z-score LME models, sex-stratified. Bold indicates p < 0.05."

require_output(
  create_lme_covariate_table(
    frs_hc, cvr_label = "FRS",
    title = "HC Covariate Effects (FRS Model)"
  ),
  "tbl-hc-covariates"
)
```

```{r tbl-hc-covariates-cvr}
#| tbl-cap: "Covariate effects from the CVR~mimic~ HC z-score LME models, sex-stratified. Bold indicates p < 0.05."

require_output(
  create_lme_covariate_table(
    cvr_hc, cvr_label = "CVR~mimic~",
    title = md(
      "HC Covariate Effects (CVR~mimic~ Model)"
    )
  ),
  "tbl-hc-covariates-cvr"
)
```

```{r hc-interpretation}
#| output: asis

stopifnot(!is.null(hc_summary.dt))
  hc_p <- hc_summary.dt[
    Analysis == "Primary_Stratified"
  ]
  for (cvr_nm in c("FRS", "CVR_mimic")) {
    cvr_rows <- hc_p[CVR_Measure == cvr_nm]
    sig_rows <- cvr_rows[Significant == TRUE]
    cvr_lab <- ifelse(
      cvr_nm == "CVR_mimic",
      "CVR~mimic~", cvr_nm
    )
    if (nrow(sig_rows) > 0) {
      sig_desc <- paste(
        sprintf(
          "%s %s",
          sig_rows$Sex,
          DOMAIN_LABELS[sig_rows$Domain]
        ),
        collapse = ", "
      )
      cat(sprintf(
        "- %s × HC × YRS: significant for %s\n",
        cvr_lab, sig_desc
      ))
    } else {
      cat(sprintf(
        "- %s × HC × YRS: no significant effects\n",
        cvr_lab
      ))
    }
  }
  cat(
    "- HC z-score showed weaker effects than HVR\n",
    "  z-score, supporting use of the ratio measure\n"
  )
```

```{r hc-vs-hvr-synthesis}
#| output: asis

# Count significant HC vs HVR effects
hc_p <- hc_summary.dt[
  Analysis == "Primary_Stratified"
]
hvr_p <- lme_summary.dt[
  Analysis == "Primary_Stratified"
]
n_hc_sig <- sum(hc_p$Significant, na.rm = TRUE)
n_hvr_sig <- sum(
  hvr_p$Significant, na.rm = TRUE
)
cat(sprintf(
  paste0(
    "- HC z-score: %d/12 significant",
    " 3-way interactions vs HVR z-score",
    " %d/12 — weaker HC effects support",
    " the specificity of HVR: capturing",
    " ventricular expansion alongside",
    " hippocampal atrophy provides",
    " greater sensitivity to CVR",
    " moderation\n"
  ),
  n_hc_sig, n_hvr_sig
))
```

### Attrition Analysis

- Baseline characteristics compared between completers (≥4 visits)
  and dropouts (<4 visits)
- Results in @tbl-attrition-baseline

```{r tbl-attrition-baseline}
#| tbl-cap: "Attrition analysis: baseline characteristics of completers (≥4 visits) vs dropouts (<4 visits). Bold rows indicate p < 0.05. Completers = participants with sufficient visits for quadratic trend estimation."

require_output(
  create_attrition_table(attrition_analysis),
  "tbl-attrition-baseline"
)
```

```{r attrition-interpretation}
#| output: asis

stopifnot(
  !is.null(attrition_analysis),
  !is.null(attrition_analysis$comparison_table)
)
{
  comp_dt <- attrition_analysis$comparison_table
  sig_vars <- comp_dt[p_value < 0.05, Variable]
  # Map variable names for readability
  var_display <- c(
    AGE = "age", EDUC = "education",
    FRS = "FRS", HVR_z = "HVR z-score",
    HC_z = "HC z-score", SEX = "sex",
    APOE4 = "APOE \u03b54 carrier status"
  )
  sig_labels <- vapply(
    sig_vars, function(v) {
      if (v %in% names(var_display)) {
        var_display[v]
      } else v
    }, character(1)
  )
  if (length(sig_vars) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf(
      paste0(
        "- Significant baseline differences",
        " between completers and dropouts",
        " in %s (all p < 0.05;",
        " see @tbl-attrition-baseline)\n"
      ),
      paste(sig_labels, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  } else {
    cat(paste0(
      "- No significant baseline",
      " differences between completers",
      " and dropouts",
      " (see @tbl-attrition-baseline),",
      " suggesting attrition was random",
      " with respect to measured",
      " variables\n"
    ))
  }
}
```

```{r attrition-logistic-inline}
#| output: asis

stopifnot(
  !is.null(attrition_analysis),
  !is.null(attrition_analysis$logistic)
)
log_model <- attrition_analysis$logistic
or_dt <- as.data.table(log_model$odds_ratios)
setnames(
  or_dt,
  c("2.5 %", "97.5 %"),
  c("CI_lower", "CI_upper"),
  skip_absent = TRUE
)
or_dt <- or_dt[term != "(Intercept)"]
or_dt[, sig := CI_lower > 1 | CI_upper < 1]

# Readable term labels
term_display <- c(
  FRS = "FRS", AGE = "age",
  APOE4TRUE = "APOE \u03b54 carrier status",
  SEXMale = "male sex",
  HVR_z = "HVR z-score",
  HC_z = "HC z-score"
)
or_dt[, term_label := fifelse(
  term %in% names(term_display),
  term_display[term], term
)]

sig_preds <- or_dt[sig == TRUE]
if (nrow(sig_preds) > 0) {
  items <- sprintf(
    "%s (OR = %.2f, 95%% CI [%.2f, %.2f])",
    sig_preds$term_label,
    sig_preds$OR,
    sig_preds$CI_lower,
    sig_preds$CI_upper
  )
  cat(sprintf(
    paste0(
      "- Logistic regression predicting",
      " dropout: %d significant",
      " predictor%s — %s\n"
    ),
    nrow(sig_preds),
    ifelse(nrow(sig_preds) > 1, "s", ""),
    paste(items, collapse = "; ")
  ))
} else {
  cat(paste0(
    "- Logistic regression predicting",
    " dropout: no significant predictors",
    " (all 95%% CIs included 1)\n"
  ))
}

# FRS direction narrative
comp_dt <- attrition_analysis$comparison_table
frs_row <- comp_dt[
  grepl("FRS", Variable, ignore.case = TRUE)
]
if (nrow(frs_row) > 0) {
  c_mean <- frs_row$Completers_mean[1]
  d_mean <- frs_row$Dropouts_mean[1]
  if (!is.null(c_mean) && !is.null(d_mean) &&
      !is.na(c_mean) && !is.na(d_mean)) {
    direction <- if (d_mean > c_mean) {
      "higher"
    } else "lower"
    cat(sprintf(
      paste0(
        "- Dropouts had %s baseline FRS",
        " (M = %.2f) than completers",
        " (M = %.2f), consistent with",
        " selective loss of higher-risk",
        " participants — may attenuate",
        " observed CVR effects\n"
      ),
      direction, d_mean, c_mean
    ))
  }
}
```

### Raw Brain Measure Sensitivity

- LME 3-way interaction repeated using raw
  (un-normalized) HVR and raw HC (with TIV
  covariate); FRS predictor, full-sample, no sex
  stratification, no YRS² term

```{r raw-hvr-inline}
#| output: asis

stopifnot(
  !is.null(lme_raw_sens.res),
  !is.null(lme_raw_sens.res$results)
)
hvr_raw <- lme_raw_sens.res$results$HVR_raw_FRS
stopifnot(!is.null(hvr_raw))
raw_hvr_results <- list(results = hvr_raw)

hc_raw <- lme_raw_sens.res$results$HC_raw_FRS
stopifnot(!is.null(hc_raw))
raw_hc_results <- list(results = hc_raw)

for (meas_nm in c("Raw HVR", "Raw HC (+TIV)")) {
  res <- if (meas_nm == "Raw HVR") {
    raw_hvr_results
  } else raw_hc_results
  items <- c()
  for (d in c("MEM", "LAN", "EXF")) {
    r <- res$results[[d]]$interaction
    if (!is.null(r)) {
      items <- c(items, sprintf(
        "%s \u03b2 = %.4f, %s",
        DOMAIN_LABELS[d], r$beta,
        fmt_p_inline(r$p_value)
      ))
    }
  }
  cat(sprintf(
    "- %s: %s\n",
    meas_nm, paste(items, collapse = "; ")
  ))
}

# Concordance check
sig_hvr <- sapply(
  names(raw_hvr_results$results),
  function(d) {
    r <- raw_hvr_results$results[[d]]$interaction
    !is.null(r) && r$p_value < 0.05
  }
)
if (any(sig_hvr)) {
  cat(sprintf(
    paste0(
      "- Raw HVR \u00d7 FRS significant for",
      " %s, confirming normative z-scoring",
      " does not drive primary findings\n"
    ),
    paste(
      DOMAIN_LABELS[names(sig_hvr)[sig_hvr]],
      collapse = ", "
    )
  ))
} else {
  cat(paste0(
    "- Raw HVR \u00d7 FRS not significant",
    " for any domain, suggesting",
    " normative z-scoring enhances",
    " sensitivity\n"
  ))
}

# Raw HC (+TIV) note
sig_hc <- sapply(
  names(raw_hc_results$results),
  function(d) {
    r <- raw_hc_results$results[[d]]$interaction
    !is.null(r) && r$p_value < 0.05
  }
)
if (!any(sig_hc)) {
  cat(paste0(
    "- Raw HC (+TIV) not significant",
    " for any domain, reinforcing the",
    " added sensitivity of the ratio",
    " measure\n"
  ))
}
```

### EDT Moderation of LME Effects

- 4-way interaction (CVR × HVR × EDT × YRS) tested
  whether CVR effects vary by disease stage

```{r edt-moderation-inline}
#| output: asis

report_edt <- function(edt_res, cvr_label) {
  stopifnot(
    !is.null(edt_res),
    !is.null(edt_res$summary_continuous)
  )
  sc <- as.data.table(edt_res$summary_continuous)
  for (sx in c("Male", "Female")) {
    sx_rows <- sc[Sex == sx]
    sig <- sx_rows[Significant == TRUE]
    if (nrow(sig) > 0) {
      items <- sprintf(
        "%s (\u03b2 = %.4f, %s)",
        DOMAIN_LABELS[sig$Domain],
        sig$Beta,
        sapply(sig$p_value, fmt_p_inline)
      )
      cat(sprintf(
        "- %s \u00d7 HVR \u00d7 EDT (%s):",
        cvr_label, sx
      ))
      cat(sprintf(
        " significant for %s\n",
        paste(items, collapse = "; ")
      ))
    } else {
      min_p <- min(
        sx_rows$p_value, na.rm = TRUE
      )
      cat(sprintf(
        paste0(
          "- %s \u00d7 HVR \u00d7 EDT (%s):",
          " not significant (min %s)\n"
        ),
        cvr_label, sx,
        fmt_p_inline(min_p)
      ))
    }
  }
}
report_edt(frs_edt, "FRS")
report_edt(cvr_edt, "CVR~mimic~")
cat(paste0(
  "- Limited EDT moderation suggests",
  " CVR effects on HVR-cognition",
  " coupling are relatively stable",
  " across disease stages\n"
))
```

### EDT Stage Sensitivity

- Analyses restricted to early and middle EDT
  tertiles (excluding late-stage) to assess
  robustness

```{r edt-sensitivity-inline}
#| output: asis

stopifnot(!is.null(edt_sens.res))
rt <- as.data.table(edt_sens.res$results_table)

meas_labels <- c(
  HVR_z = "HVR z-score",
  HC_z = "HC z-score"
)
dom_labels <- c(
  MEM = "Memory", LAN = "Language",
  EXF = "Executive Function"
)

for (meas in unique(rt$Measure)) {
  mrt <- rt[Measure == meas]
  m_lab <- if (meas %in% names(meas_labels)) {
    meas_labels[meas]
  } else meas
  items <- c()
  n_concord <- 0L
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(mrt))) {
    row <- mrt[i]
    d_lab <- if (
      row$Domain %in% names(dom_labels)
    ) dom_labels[row$Domain] else row$Domain
    pct <- if (
      !is.null(row$Beta_change_pct) &&
      !is.na(row$Beta_change_pct)
    ) {
      sprintf(
        ", %.1f%% change",
        row$Beta_change_pct
      )
    } else ""
    if (!is.null(row$Consistent) &&
        isTRUE(row$Consistent)) {
      n_concord <- n_concord + 1L
    }
    items <- c(items, sprintf(
      "%s (\u03b2 = %.4f, %s%s)",
      d_lab, row$Beta_early_middle,
      fmt_p_inline(row$P_early_middle),
      pct
    ))
  }
  concord_note <- if (n_concord > 0) {
    sprintf(
      " (%d/%d concordant with full-sample)",
      n_concord, nrow(mrt)
    )
  } else ""
  cat(sprintf(
    "- %s: %s%s\n",
    m_lab,
    paste(items, collapse = "; "),
    concord_note
  ))
}
```

:::{=latex}
\FloatBarrier
:::

---

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
